steps.label: cause
steps.text: Mechanical harvesting can break berries before they reach the winery.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: When berries are broken, the must is exposed to oxygen before processing, which can begin premature oxidation of aromas and increase phenolic extraction.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The finished wine can show reduced aromatic freshness and potentially greater extraction of phenolic compounds compared with carefully handled intact fruit.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Mechanical harvesting can reduce aromatic freshness because berry breakage exposes must to oxygen before winemaking; this increases oxidation risk unless handling is rapid and protective.
